title: Abbott Laboratories (ABT) Intrinsic Value — Opus 4.6 Analysis
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Abbott Laboratories (ABT).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Abbott Laboratories is projected to grow FCF at 8% annually based on competitive dynamics and historical performance.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 8.5% WACC reflects Abbott Laboratories's risk profile, sector volatility, and balance sheet quality.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal rate for long-run growth convergence toward nominal GDP.

thesisParagraphs: Abbott Laboratories (ABT) is an S&P 500 constituent with established competitive advantages and consistent cash flow generation. The company's market position provides pricing power and earnings stability.

My DCF applies a 8% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability, with a 8.5% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks. The valuation is sensitive to growth assumptions — investors should stress-test these inputs.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Abbott Laboratories's intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 8% growth and 8.5% WACC, Opus values ABT at $120.49/share vs $125.00 current price.

faq.question: Is ABT overvalued or undervalued?
faq.answer: Opus's DCF suggests ABT is fair value with a -3.6% margin of safety.

faq.question: Should I trade based on this?
faq.answer: No. This is not financial advice. DCF models are sensitive to assumptions. Use as one data point among many.
